idea.hook: Earth isn't the only world with vast oceans! 🌊
facts: Jupiter's moon Europa: Its subsurface ocean contains more than twice the volume of all Earth's oceans combined.
Saturn's moon Enceladus: Regularly blasts geysers of saltwater from its south pole, hinting at a warm, active ocean beneath its icy crust.
Dwarf planet Ceres: Data suggests it harbors a significant deep brine reservoir beneath its largest impact basin, potentially over 40 km thick.
idea.payoff: Our Solar System might be teeming with more liquid water than we thought, hidden in unexpected places, potentially harboring life!
idea.title: Hidden Oceans of Our Solar System! 🤯

scenes.text: Earth isn't the only world with vast oceans! 🌊
scenes.visual: A vibrant blue Earth with swirling oceans, then a subtle transition to show a partially obscured, icy celestial body in the foreground.

scenes.text: Europa: Subsurface ocean >2x Earth's oceans combined!
scenes.visual: Jupiter's moon Europa. A cross-section animation reveals a thick ice crust with a massive liquid ocean beneath, dwarfing a small representation of Earth's oceans.

scenes.text: Enceladus: Blasts saltwater geysers from its warm, active ocean!
scenes.visual: Saturn's moon Enceladus, with bright, icy geysers erupting from its south pole, visually implying a hidden heat source and ocean.

scenes.text: Ceres: Deep brine reservoir, over 40 km thick!
scenes.visual: Dwarf planet Ceres, focusing on its largest impact basin. An animated cutaway shows a deep, thick layer of brine beneath the surface.

scenes.text: Our Solar System: Teeming with hidden water, potentially harboring life! ✨
scenes.visual: A cosmic panorama showing Europa, Enceladus, and Ceres orbiting in distant space. Subtle sparkling effects emanate from within each, representing hidden water and the potential for life, ending with a wide shot of the mysterious solar system.

youtube.title: Hidden Oceans of Our Solar System! 🤯
